articles.title: Rationing power and diluting petrol - how African countries are coping with effects of Iran war
articles.summary: The disruption in oil supplies has caused shortages and concerns in several African countries.

articles.title: Panic buying prompts PM to reassure Australians over fuel supply
articles.summary: Anthony Albanese says nation's supply remains "secure" amid reports of panic buying and shortages.

articles.title: Two Cuba-bound aid ships missing after leaving Mexico
articles.summary: Mexico's navy launches a rescue mission for ships delivering aid to Cuba, strained by food and fuel shortages amid a US embargo.

articles.title: Belarus leader gifts rifle to North Korea's Kim as they sign friendship treaty
articles.summary: Two key supporters of Russia's war in Ukraine close ranks as Alexander Lukashenko visits Pyongyang.

articles.title: Women secretly filmed, then ridiculed and abused online
articles.summary: Women in Kenya and Ghana tell the BBC about being approached by a Russian man who later posts videos of them without their consent.

articles.title: Trump's signature to appear on US dollars in first for sitting president
articles.summary: The US president's signature will appear on new paper currency alongside Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent.

articles.title: Sarah Ferguson's 'close Epstein ties' scrutinised in US lawmaker's letter
articles.summary: The letter, seen by the BBC, also called on Ferguson to share any knowledge about Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor's links to Epstein.

articles.title: Judge rejects Pentagon's attempt to 'cripple' Anthropic
articles.summary: A federal judge told the government it could not immediately enforce a ban on Anthropic’s tools.

articles.title: Spanish woman dies by euthanasia after long legal battle with father
articles.summary: The European Court of Human Rights (ECHR) ruled in Noelia Castillo's favour earlier this week.

articles.title: Orban's Hungarian government accused of mass voter intimidation ahead of election
articles.summary: A film quoting voters, mayors and a police officer alleges money and drugs are being offered to pressure people to back the ruling party.
